name: clean-up-remaining-20260406-0428
idea: Clean up remaining engineering debt from the shot creation atomic RPC migration. Four items: (1) createShotWithFilesPath still does two roundtrips (upload files, then fetch shot_generations to hydrate cache). Create an atomic create_shot_with_files Supabase RPC or at minimum have the upload pipeline return complete generation data so the post-upload fetch is unnecessary. (2) useDuplicateAsNewGeneration collision resolution logic (midpoint calculation, +30 offset, retry up to 1000) now exists in BOTH the SQL RPC (duplicate_as_new_generation) AND TypeScript (the old helpers may still exist or the logic is duplicated for UI preview). Consolidate to SQL-only — the client should just call the RPC and trust the returned frame. Remove any remaining TypeScript collision resolution code that duplicates the SQL. (3) ClipAction snapshot pattern (shotActionsSnapshotRef) works around EffectRegistry re-renders causing canCreateShotFromSelection to flicker. Root fix: investigate why EffectRegistry re-renders cause selectionShotCreationState to recompute with canCreateShot=false. Likely the registry or data object reference changes on every effect registration cycle even when the actual clip/generation data hasn't changed. Fix with proper memoization so the snapshot workaround can be removed. (4) Node.contains() returns false for portal children in ClipContextMenu and SelectionContextMenu, worked around with stopPropagation on the menu div. Investigate root cause — likely React portal reconciliation or ref timing issue. If unfixable, at least document the workaround clearly and ensure it's applied consistently.

history.message: execute output missing required keys: output, files_changed, commands_run, deviations
execute output missing required keys: output, files_changed, commands_run, deviations

overrides.note: User guidance: think through implications carefully before executing. For item 2 (collision resolution), keep calculateDuplicateFrame for legitimate client-side preview callers but document that SQL is authoritative. For item 1 (files path), assess which approach is simpler during planning. For item 3 (EffectRegistry), try splitting the data dependency first (option B). For item 4 (portal contains), if root cause is unfixable, document the workaround consistently.
